name: Optimize CI time for Shopify CLI (slowest check: Unit tests on Windows)

description: Baseline: slowest CI job is Unit tests Node 20 on Windows at 8.7m. Windows jobs: 8.7m/8.3m/8.5m. Ubuntu ~4.6-4.9m. macOS ~3.8-5.5m. Test Coverage 7.4m. Local: 77s.
Lighter reporter in CI (default vs verbose) + 2-way sharding for Windows. Slowest job now Test Coverage 6.8m (was 8.7m). Windows shards: 4.5-6.4m (was 8.3-8.7m). Local: 63s (was 77s).
Remove build from test-coverage using coverage.all=false. Test Coverage 6.5m (was 6.9m). Build step no longer needed since all=false doesn't instrument untested files. All green.

Fix coverage blob merge (unique filenames). All green. Slowest unit test: macOS Node 20 at 5.2m. Coverage merge works correctly (0.8m merge step). graphql-codegen had a slow run at 6.4m (runner variance).
Final CI run confirmation. All green. Typical slowest ~5.1-5.5m (macOS 6.1m was runner variance). Coverage stable at 4.2m. All checks pass including coverage merge and PR comment.
Final stable state. All green. Reverted graphql-codegen to macOS. Slowest: Windows Node 20 shard 2/2 at 5.6m. Coverage: 3.5m shard + 0.8m merge. All checks pass.

Switch to basic reporter in CI. All green. Slowest: macOS Node 20 at 5.5m (runner variance). Windows improved slightly (5.0/4.8m vs 5.2/4.8m). Local: 72s vs 82s (12% faster). Keeping for local dev speed benefit.
Remove ui-extensions-test-utils from vitest workspace (0 tests). Local: 66s (was 72s, -8%). CI: 5.3m slowest (Win N24 shard 1). Most windows improved to 4.2-5.3m. All green.

Merge main, remove stale acceptance-tests job, add --prefer-offline. All green. Slowest unit: 5.4m (Win N24 shard 1). --prefer-offline had no measurable effect (cache already warm). Install times unchanged.
Add node_modules caching (cache miss run). Slowest: 6m (Win N20 shard 2/2) — slower due to cache save overhead. All green. Next push should show cache hit benefit.
